title: Surface Tension
description: Adds a few lively decorations for your ocean-facing and under-water homes.
body:
![[Surface Tension Title]](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a705ff60659cee9d6174f57b8a52c0c415733efa_0.webp)

<center> A small mod that aims to expand Minecraft's oceans

  ---

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/ALkCzTDHRqs" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

  </center>

  # Features
- Bubbles appear in underwater caves for air replenishment.
- Crab claw item that exends block placing and breaking reach when held.

# Blocks
- Tension rod - Crafted from copper blocks and its' variants. can be used to prevent water from flowing downwards in a 3x3 grid.
- Geyser Blocks - Sand and gravel variants that emit bubble entites when below water. Unaffected by gravity.
- Hook - A decorative block that can be used to dry cod and salmon fishes when placed above campfires. dried fishes give 10 seconds of Water Breathing upon consumption.
- Net - A decorative block which allows any entity to travel up through it, but blocks movement down.
  
- Plankton Bush - An underwater plant that emits "plankton" particles around it. Barnacle clusters placed 1 block below it in a 3x3 grid will grow after some time had passed.
- Barnacles - A decorative block found in buried ship chests. Can be placed on all sides of the block, and has three cluster sizes. Place a barnacle on a barnacle cluster to add to it's size.
  
- Dock Post - A decorative block.
- Dock Net - A decorative block. Connects to dock posts.
- Coral Shelf & Dead Coral Shelf - Decorational blocks. Generate in ocean biomes. act similarly to minecraft's corals.

# Mobs
- Bubble - Slowly rises in water until it hits something or leaves the water. players can collide with it in order to replenish breathing time.
  
- Crab - The crab from minecraft's mob vote. Spawns in mangrove forests. Use shears to collect its' large claw.

---
